notes.anchor: deputy in the contracts for the pasturage tax
notes.note: Pro magistro in scriptura: the on-site manager (one rank below the magister) of the publican company that held the contract for the scriptura, the tax on the use of public pasture-lands across the Asian provinces. The standing was equestrian; the work, settlement of fixed-sum agreements (pactiones) with each city.

Pactiones: in this context the negotiated agreements between the publican company and each Greek city, fixing a lump-sum payment in lieu of the variable tax-yield. Concluding them was the deputy's central task and the measure of his standing.

In mea fide: a near-formula --- the tax-farming partnership has formally been taken into Cicero's fides, the relationship of patronage and protection. Cicero often took whole publican societates under his fides during his career as advocate.
